ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: A nyaaŋa daba maa yeli, “Pimparaŋa wune m pa nye m gbagba ŋmaani, m koba niŋi kobiri, m valiga niŋi valiga. O n yi daba niŋi zu, o yuuri n niŋi poga.”
translated: One day, the mouse said, "When I am in the farm, I am a farmer, when I am at the river, I am a fisherman, and when I am in the bush, I am a hunter."

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Dindaso wuntonkpaligu, Duuma daa n ta Ibrahim saani, a leeli Mamiri titintaasi. Ka Ibrahim daa n zinni o gbana sugiri noori. Jemanso zii n bii.
translated: The chief of the village, Duuma, had a son named Ibrahim, who was very handsome. But Ibrahim had a very bad temper. The villagers were afraid of him.

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Loti daa n zorini dabeem, a kpali o n zi Zowa la zu, o ni o bipunsi bayi maa yi tiŋŋa maa puu zo n wa ti ba zovoori puwa.
translated: When Lot and his family got up early in the morning to leave, they bowed down before the men of the area and said they were going to the mountain to worship.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: Isimeel baligu maa daa zinni Havila ni Suu sunsuuniŋi, Yijipiti wuluŋpusigu, Asiriya soori. Ba ni ba niriba daa n bi lagimi n zi. Isawu ni Yakubu dogim yela
translated: And it came to pass, as he drew back his hand, that behold, his brother came out, and she said, How did you get such a breach, or wherefore has the breach been broken upon you, and she called his name Breach, and afterward his brother came out, that had the red thread upon his hand, and his name was called Zerah, or the Eastern one, or the Rising one, and Jacob was the father of them

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Duuma malaaka wa ti paa Heega, a yi bulugu n ba Sora soori, tambisigu tiŋgbaŋŋu niŋi.
translated: The angel of the Lord came and sat under the oak tree in Ophrah, and his sons were with him in the field
